pr.title: Cleanup Samus, Zelda, and Yoshi's data pointers, plus some ASCII
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned up asm/data.s by replacing many raw numeric .4byte addresses and hex-encoded ASCII words with labels, .asciz strings, and symbolized pointer tables for Samus, Yoshi, Zelda, and a few generic debug/assertion strings. The PR appears to be a data readability and relocation/symbolization cleanup with no C source changes and no recorded review discussion.
what_changed: Converted raw .4byte address constants in top-level data pointer tables to label references, for example 0x803CE6D0 to lbl_803CE6D0, 0x803CFEF0 to lbl_803CFEF0, 0x803CE510 to lbl_803CE510, and 0x803CFC98 to lbl_803CFC98.

Recovered Samus filename and data strings from packed 32-bit hex words into explicit .asciz labels, including "PlSs.dat", "ftDataSamus", color costume files such as "PlSsNr.dat", "PlSsPi.dat", "PlSsBk.dat", "PlSsGr.dat", "PlSsLa.dat", and AJ/demo strings such as "ftDemoResultMotionFileSamus".

Introduced labeled Samus string and pointer-table symbols such as lbl_803CE510, lbl_803CE51C, lbl_803CE528, lbl_803CE534, lbl_803CE668, and lbl_803CE678, using .balign 4 after strings and label references inside pointer arrays.

Recovered Yoshi data strings and tables into labels, including "PlYs.dat", "ftDataYoshi", color files "PlYsNr.dat", "PlYsRe.dat", "PlYsBu.dat", "PlYsYe.dat", "PlYsPi.dat", "PlYsAq.dat", "PlYsAJ.dat", joint strings, matanim joint strings, and demo motion file strings.
Added Yoshi pointer table labels such as lbl_803CECEC for demo motion file pointers and lbl_803CECFC for costume resource pointer entries.

Recovered Zelda data strings and tables into labels, including "PlZd.dat", "ftDataZelda", "PlZdNr.dat", "PlZdRe.dat", "PlZdBu.dat", "PlZdGr.dat", "PlZdWh.dat", "PlZdAJ.dat", joint/matanim joint names, and demo motion file strings.
Added Zelda pointer table labels such as lbl_803CFEA0 for demo motion file pointers and lbl_803CFEB0 for costume resource pointer entries.

Replaced packed hex ASCII debug strings near lbl_8040C360 and related labels with readable .asciz strings: "Particle:setBlendMode:Unknown mode
", "psdisp.c", "Out CameraDisp Range
", "ZERO byte alloc
", "sislib.c", and "Memory Empty
".
Removed an inline comment reading "TODO: What is this table?" from one pointer-table area while symbolizing nearby entries.
smart_moves: Preserved binary layout while improving readability by pairing every recovered .asciz string with .balign 4, matching the prior word-aligned packed .4byte representation.
Separated contiguous string blobs from pointer arrays by placing explicit labels at string starts and table starts, making the data easier to reference from future C structs or headers.
Converted address-valued .4byte entries to label references only where corresponding labels were established, reducing magic addresses without changing table semantics.

Grouped recovered fighter resources in recognizable patterns: base fighter data file, ftData name, costume file names, joint resources, matanim joint resources where applicable, AJ file, and demo motion file name table.
Used existing address-derived lbl_ naming rather than inventing semantic symbol names, which is appropriate for an assembly cleanup stage when full type/function ownership is not yet established.
decomp_lessons: Packed 32-bit hex words in data.s often encode null-terminated ASCII strings; decoding them to .asciz plus .balign 4 makes later structure recovery much easier while preserving alignment.
When raw addresses inside tables point into nearby data blobs, add labels at the targets and replace numeric constants with .4byte label references to expose table structure.

Fighter resource data tends to follow repeatable naming and table patterns across characters; Samus, Yoshi, and Zelda examples show reusable layouts for costume files, joint names, matanim names, AJ files, and demo motion-file lists.
Do not over-name unknown data prematurely: this PR retained lbl_803... labels instead of guessing final C symbol names, but still improved searchability and pointer relationships.
ASCII cleanup can be mixed with pointer cleanup as long as alignment and table boundaries are made explicit; .balign 4 is essential after strings that were previously represented as word data.
naming_conventions: Temporary data labels use address-based names in the form lbl_803CE510, lbl_803CE678, lbl_803CFEB0, etc.

Fighter data filenames use short internal character/file prefixes such as PlSs for Samus, PlYs for Yoshi, and PlZd for Zelda, followed by costume suffixes like Nr, Pi, Bk, Gr, La, Re, Bu, Ye, Aq, Wh, and AJ.
Fighter data symbol strings use ftData<Character>, such as ftDataSamus, ftDataYoshi, and ftDataZelda.
Joint resource names follow Ply<Character>5K<Color>_Share_joint, for example PlySamus5KPi_Share_joint, PlyYoshi5KRe_Share_joint, and PlyZelda5KWh_Share_joint.
Characters with material animation resources use Ply<Character>5K<Color>_Share_matanim_joint, as shown for Yoshi and Zelda.
Demo motion file strings follow ftDemo<Result|Intro|Ending|ViWait>MotionFile<Character>, such as ftDemoResultMotionFileSamus and ftDemoViWaitMotionFileZelda.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Decode hex ASCII .4byte sequences into .asciz strings and add .balign 4 to retain word alignment.
Replace numeric address constants with symbolic label references in .4byte tables once the targets have explicit labels.
Create labels for both string entries and sub-tables embedded inside larger data regions, such as separate labels for demo motion file pointer arrays and costume resource arrays.
Keep NULL entries as explicit .4byte NULL inside pointer arrays to preserve terminators and table holes.
Use .global before newly exposed labels in asm/data.s so other assembly references and future C declarations can refer to them.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace lbl_803CE678, lbl_803CECFC, or lbl_803CFEB0 with typed fighter data structs.
Search for additional packed ASCII in asm/data.s that can be converted to .asciz with .balign 4.
Compare Samus, Yoshi, and Zelda resource pointer table layouts against other fighter data tables for reusable struct definitions.
Investigate whether the removed TODO table near lbl_803C1F40 was later identified and named.
